title: Benedict Arnold's Birthday
body: Benedict Arnold was born on January 14, 1741. His name became shorthand for betrayal in American history, which is dramatic enough to make any family birthday look peacefully uncomplicated.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Today Show Begins
body: NBC's Today show premiered on January 14, 1952. It helped shape the morning television format, which means this date has been waking people up for decades.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: India's Republic Day
body: India's constitution came into effect on January 26, 1950, and the date is now celebrated as Republic Day. It is one of the country's major civic holidays.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Wayne Gretzky's Birthday
body: Wayne Gretzky was born on January 26, 1961. The hockey world still calls him The Great One, which is a pretty efficient nickname.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Jules Verne's Birthday
body: Jules Verne was born on February 8, 1828. His adventure novels helped generations imagine submarines, moon trips, and faraway journeys before those ideas felt ordinary.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Boy Scouts of America Incorporated
body: The Boy Scouts of America was incorporated on February 8, 1910. The date has a built-in outdoorsy, merit-badge kind of energy.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Marian Anderson's Birthday
body: Marian Anderson was born on February 27, 1897. Her voice and career helped open doors in American classical music and public life.
source_name: National Museum of African American History and Culture
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Joanne Woodward's Birthday
body: Joanne Woodward was born on February 27, 1930. Her acting career brought another classic Hollywood note to a date already rich with famous birthdays.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Colin Powell's Birthday
body: Colin Powell was born on April 5, 1937. He became a four-star general, national security adviser, and secretary of state.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Bette Davis's Birthday
body: Bette Davis was born on April 5, 1908. Her screen presence made her one of classic Hollywood's most memorable performers.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Samuel Morse's Birthday
body: Samuel F. B. Morse was born on April 27, 1791. His name became attached to Morse code, which made dots and dashes famous long before texting shortcuts existed.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Lizzo's Birthday
body: Lizzo was born on April 27, 1988. Her birthday gives the date a modern music note with plenty of confidence and sparkle.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Lewis and Clark Set Out
body: The Lewis and Clark Expedition set out from Camp Dubois on May 14, 1804. The journey became one of the best-known exploration stories in American history.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: George Lucas's Birthday
body: George Lucas was born on May 14, 1944. His movies gave the calendar lightsabers, droids, and a very reusable reason to say, May the Force be with you.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Canada Day
body: July 1 is Canada Day, marking the 1867 confederation of Canada. It gives the date flags, fireworks, and a very polite national celebration.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Sony Walkman Arrives
body: Sony released the first Walkman in Japan on July 1, 1979. It helped make music feel portable and personal long before phones took over the job.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Nikola Tesla's Birthday
body: Nikola Tesla was born on July 10, 1856. His work with electricity gave the date a spark of invention that still feels futuristic.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Wyoming Becomes a State
body: Wyoming became the 44th U.S. state on July 10, 1890. The date brings a bit of wide-open western history to the birthday calendar.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Bastille Day
body: July 14 is Bastille Day in France, marking a major symbol of the French Revolution. It gives the date a built-in parade-and-fireworks feeling.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Woody Guthrie's Birthday
body: Woody Guthrie was born on July 14, 1912. His songs became part of American folk music's backbone.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Apollo 11 Launches
body: Apollo 11 launched from Kennedy Space Center on July 16, 1969. The mission would carry the first humans to the Moon, giving this date a huge space-history headline.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Ida B. Wells's Birthday
body: Ida B. Wells was born on July 16, 1862. She became a pioneering journalist, activist, and organizer.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Disneyland Opens
body: Disneyland opened in Anaheim, California, on July 17, 1955. The date has been associated with rides, castles, and very strategic snack planning ever since.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Angela Merkel's Birthday
body: Angela Merkel was born on July 17, 1954. She later served as Germany's chancellor and became one of Europe's most prominent political figures.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Tony Bennett's Birthday
body: Tony Bennett was born on August 3, 1926. His voice gave the date a timeless, tuxedo-ready soundtrack.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Jesse Owens Wins Olympic Gold
body: Jesse Owens won the Olympic 100 meters in Berlin on August 3, 1936. It was one of the defining athletic moments of the 20th century.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: IBM Introduces the Personal Computer
body: IBM introduced its first personal computer on August 12, 1981. The machine helped make the phrase PC part of everyday life.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Sue the T. rex Is Discovered
body: The fossil later known as Sue the T. rex was discovered on August 12, 1990. It became one of the most famous dinosaur specimens in the world.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: National Aviation Day
body: August 19 is National Aviation Day in the United States. It lines up with Orville Wright's birthday and gives the date a built-in wings-and-runways theme.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Orville Wright's Birthday
body: Orville Wright was born on August 19, 1871. Along with his brother Wilbur, he helped turn the dream of powered flight into history.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Lyndon B. Johnson's Birthday
body: Lyndon B. Johnson was born on August 27, 1908. He later became the 36th president of the United States.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The Guinness Book of Records Is Published
body: The first Guinness Book of Records was published on August 27, 1955. It gave people a very official reason to argue about the fastest, tallest, smallest, and strangest things.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Keanu Reeves's Birthday
body: Keanu Reeves was born on September 2, 1964. His film career gives the date action, kindness memes, and a surprising amount of internet goodwill.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: The U.S. Treasury Department Is Established
body: The United States Department of the Treasury was established on September 2, 1789. That gives the date an unexpectedly practical money-and-government footnote.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: United Nations Day
body: October 24 is United Nations Day, marking the 1945 entry into force of the UN Charter. It gives the date a global cooperation theme.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.

title: Sarah Josepha Hale's Birthday
body: Sarah Josepha Hale was born on October 24, 1788. She is remembered as an editor, writer, and major advocate for Thanksgiving becoming a national holiday.
notes: Birthday-date supplemental fact; verify exact source/date during later data hardening.
